summary: Iteration 2 review complete. Rewrote backend tests for new manufacturer + generic shipment schema (24/24 pytest passing). Frontend end-to-end UI flows verified — landing 3-role tiles with Manufacturer default + Unilever auto-selected, role-aware sidebars (Mfg has Distributors+no Requests; Distributor has Retailers+Requests; Retailer has Requests only), all main pages render (Dashboard, Inventory, Shipments, Network, Analytics, Reports), 'New shipment' dialog opens for manufacturer with distributor picker, Switch account returns to landing.

minor.issue: retailers_count = db.retailers.count_documents({}) counts ALL retailers globally regardless of manufacturer. Works fine for single-manufacturer (Unilever) but breaks multi-manufacturer correctness. Should filter by retailers whose distributor.manufacturer_id == entity_id.
Inventory adjustment clamps at 0 with no warning. Known/accepted business rule per problem statement.

issues: Recharts emits width(-1)/height(-1) console warnings during route transitions (non-blocking, known per problem statement).

action_items: Optional: filter retailers_count by manufacturer's distributors in /api/analytics for multi-manufacturer correctness.
Optional: split server.py (~916 lines) into focused routers (entities, shipments, requests, analytics, reports, seed) to satisfy the >700-line guideline.
Optional: silence Recharts width(-1)/height(-1) warnings by wrapping ResponsiveContainer with explicit min-height/min-width or stable parent dimensions.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py exceeds 700-line size guideline (~916 lines) — split into modules.
analytics retailers_count for manufacturer ignores manufacturer scope (count_documents({})) — correct for current data but a latent bug.
_seed_from_csv prints no log on row parse errors; silently skips malformed retailer rows (ValueError on quantity fields handled, but no observability).

Shipment.from_role/to_role accept any PartyRole pair — no server-side guard against nonsensical combinations like manufacturer→retailer or retailer→manufacturer. Consider validating allowed transitions at create time.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_supply_chain.py (replaced with iteration-2 schema tests; 24 tests covering manufacturers, generic shipments, mfg→dist + dist→retailer lifecycles, analytics with distributors_count/retailers_count, reports From/To columns, seed counts 1/91/91/15/>=12)

success_rate.frontend: ~95% (all critical flows pass; one timing-related assertion on distributor nav-retailers count failed in script but verified visually via screenshot)

seed_data_creation: Used POST /api/seed (already idempotent). Confirmed counts: manufacturers=1 (Unilever), distributors=91, retailers=91, products=15, shipments=12, inventory_rows=2745. primary_distributor_id and primary_retailer_id returned by seed endpoint and used in tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file is at /app/backend/tests/test_supply_chain.py (pytest, 24 tests, all green). Iteration 1 sample numbers (e.g. distributors>=2) replaced with hard counts (91/91/15). Use seed body's primary_distributor_id/primary_retailer_id for any flow needing a distributor that actually has at least one retailer attached. Verified visually that DistributorDashboard 'Connected Retailers' shows 0 initially while async fetch resolves — this is just a render-on-mount snapshot, not a bug. Frontend screenshots in .screenshots/ show role-aware sidebar correct for Mfg and Distributor.
rca_of_the_issue: No active bugs to RCA. Minor analytics scoping observation noted above.
